channel.title: You Are Here with E.J. Kalafarski
channel.description: Recent content on You Are Here with E.J. Kalafarski

item.title: Three Laws for Living With AI
item.description:
We may not be at the point we need Laws of Robotics yet, but we’re reaching the point where we need some laws for living with AI.

I’m not an AI doomsayer (at least, not yet). AI will solve problems that would take us hundreds of years (like finding new medicines or gene sequences), and it will free up time for humans to be creative and together.

But alongside AI, we need laws to live with it without losing the essence of creativity—it’s here to assist us, not to replace us:

item.title: The Democrats’ Project 2029 Has To Go Big
item.description:
Democrats are pitching their version of Project 2025. It appears to suck so far.

If Dems want to compete with the kind of platform that Trump swept into his second term with, they need big ideas that are dead simple to understand. Zohran Mamdani’s platform was “free pre-K, free buses, tax the weathly.” That’s something you can remember in a voting booth. Mild platitudes about addressing affordable housing or reducing annoyances aren‘t going to cut it.
